leadText: The AuroraTicket (Japanese: オーロラチケット Aurora Ticket) is a Key Item introduced in Generation III.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The AuroraTicket allows the player to access Birth Island , an island hidden among the Sevii Islands . There, the player can find, battle, and attempt to capture Deoxys . Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen [ edit | edit source ] This ticket is accepted by the Seagallop . The player must have obtained the Rainbow Pass before using the AuroraTicket. Pokémon Emerald [ edit | edit source ] This ticket is accepted by the S.S. Tidal in Lilycove City . The player must have obtained the S.S. Ticket before using the AuroraTicket.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description FR LG A ticket required to board the ship to Birth Island . It glows beautifully. E A ticket required to board the ship to Birth Island .

Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method FR LG ( NS ) Pallet Town (appears in the player's Bag after entering the Hall of Fame ) Distribution [ edit | edit source ] A flyer for Pokémon Day 2005 , containing instructions for getting the AuroraTicket Main article: List of other event distributions in Generation III → AuroraTicket The AuroraTicket was distributed during various Pokémon-related events and occasions in Japan, the United States, Mexico, and select European countries. All possible language and version combinations of Pokémon Emerald and Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen were capable of obtaining this event, with the sole exception of Emerald's Japanese copies. Due to all Japanese-compatible AuroraTicket distributions taking place between April 17 to August 31, 2004 (before Emerald was released, the distribution software at that time only had support for FireRed and LeafGreen), and all subsequent distributions being incompatible with Japanese game cartridges, Japanese language copies of Emerald were not capable of receiving this event. [ 1 ] In the Nintendo Switch re-release of Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen, the AuroraTicket no longer requires an event distribution as in the original games. Instead, it automatically appears in the player's Bag once the player has become Champion and entered the Hall of Fame . Electronic Game Show Giveaway [ edit | edit source ] In Mexico, the AuroraTicket was distributed during the Electronic Game Show 2004, a video game convention that took place at the World Trade Center of Mexico City from October 22 to 24. The distribution event was a joint collaboration between Club Nintendo Mexico, Nintendo of America and Pokémex. To get the Ticket, players had to visit the Pokémon booth in the convention with their Game Boy Advance , a cartridge of Pokémon FireRed or LeafGreen and a Game Boy Advance Wireless Adapter . ↑ https://github.com/kwsch/PKHeX/issues/3040
